name: ModernGL (Modern OpenGL for Python)

auth.notes: Local library; no auth required; requires OpenGL 3.3+ capable GPU

performance.notes: Direct OpenGL; GPU-accelerated; compute shaders for GPGPU

agent_readiness.notes: Clean Modern OpenGL (3.3+) wrapper. import moderngl. ctx = moderngl.create_context(). prog = ctx.program(vertex_shader=VERT_GLSL, fragment_shader=FRAG_GLSL). vbo = ctx.buffer(np.array([...], dtype='f4').tobytes()). vao = ctx.vertex_array(prog, [(vbo, '3f', 'in_vert')]). fbo = ctx.framebuffer(color_attachments=[ctx.texture((800, 600), 4)]). fbo.use(). vao.render(moderngl.TRIANGLES). Image: data = fbo.read(components=4). Compute: ctx.compute_shader(COMPUTE_GLSL). Transforms: ctx.transform. Standalone context: moderngl.create_standalone_context(). No windowing — use pygame, GLFW, or Qt for window.

gotchas: GLSL shader knowledge required — not beginner-friendly
No built-in windowing — must pair with pygame/glfw/Qt for display
OpenGL 3.3+ required — older hardware/VMs may not support
Context must be created before any ModernGL operations
Error messages from GLSL compilation can be cryptic
best_when: Custom GPU rendering and GPGPU compute shaders in Python
Scientific visualization with custom OpenGL shaders
Offscreen rendering for image processing pipelines
When PyOpenGL is too verbose and PyVista is too high-level
avoid_when: Complete 3D engine needed (use Panda3D or Godot)
summary: ModernGL wraps Modern OpenGL (3.3+) cleanly. create_context(). program(vertex_shader, fragment_shader). buffer(numpy_data). vertex_array(prog, [(vbo, '3f', 'in_vert')]). framebuffer() for offscreen. compute_shader(). Standalone headless context. MIT by Zsolt Tóth. Best for custom GPU rendering and compute shaders. GLSL knowledge required. No windowing (pair with pygame/glfw). Cleaner than PyOpenGL.
